devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । प्रजापतेर्दुहितरं शिशुमारस्य वै ध्रुवः । उपयेमे भ्रमिं नाम तत्सुतौ कल्पवत्सरौ ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca prajāpaterduhitaraṃ śiśumārasya vai dhruvaḥ upayeme bhramiṃ nāma tatsutau kalpavatsarau

translation:
Maitreya said:
Dhruva married Bhrami, the daughter of Prajāpati Śiśumāra. They had two sons, Kalpa and Vatsara.

devanagari: इलायामपि भार्यायां वायोः पुत्र्यां महाबलः । पुत्रं उत्कलनामानं योषिद् रत्‍नमजीजनत् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: ilāyāmapi bhāryāyāṃ vāyoḥ putryāṃ mahābalaḥ putraṃ utkalanāmānaṃ yoṣid rat‍namajījanat
translation: And by his second wife Ilā, the daughter of Vāyu, the mighty Dhruva begot a son by name Utkala who was covetable to women like a jewel.[1]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
yoṣid-ratna [ratnam]—(he got) a daughter who was like a jewel among women—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā Padaratnāvalī accepts the v.l. yogeśvaram [yogeśvara]—(the son Utkala) who was the master of Yoga and possessed supernatural powers (siddhis like aṇimā).

devanagari: उत्तमस्त्वकृतोद्वाहो मृगयायां बलीयसा । हतः पुण्यजनेनाद्रौ तन्मातास्य गतिं गता ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: uttamastvakṛtodvāho mṛgayāyāṃ balīyasā hataḥ puṇyajanenādrau tanmātāsya gatiṃ gatā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: But Uttama, who was yet unmarried, was killed in a hunting expedition, in the Himālayas, by a more powerful Yakṣa. His mother, Suruci, also followed his course.

devanagari: ध्रुवो भ्रातृवधं श्रुत्वा कोपामर्षशुचार्पितः । जैत्रं स्यन्दनमास्थाय गतः पुण्यजनालयम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: dhruvo bhrātṛvadhaṃ śrutvā kopāmarṣaśucārpitaḥ jaitraṃ syandanamāsthāya gataḥ puṇyajanālayam
translation: Having heard of his brother’s death, Dhruva was filled with wrath, revenge and sorrow. Mounting his victorious chariot, he marched against the abode of Yakṣas, the city of Alakā.

devanagari: गत्वोदीचीं दिशं राजा रुद्रानुचरसेविताम् । ददर्श हिमवद्द्रोण्यां पुरीं गुह्यकसंकुलाम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: gatvodīcīṃ diśaṃ rājā rudrānucarasevitām dadarśa himavaddroṇyāṃ purīṃ guhyakasaṃkulām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Proceeding to the northern direction, he saw, in a valley of the Himālayas, the city, Alakā, crowded with Yakṣas, and frequented by the followers of Rudra (Such as goblins, spirits).

devanagari: दध्मौ शङ्खं बृहद्‍बाहुः खं दिशश्चानुनादयन् । येनोद्विग्नदृशः क्षत्तः उपदेव्योऽत्रसन्भृशम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: dadhmau śaṅkhaṃ bṛhad‍bāhuḥ khaṃ diśaścānunādayan yenodvignadṛśaḥ kṣattaḥ upadevyo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: The stout-armed Dhruva blew his conch, resounding the sky and all the quarters. Oh Vidura, thereby the demigoddesses (women of the Yakṣa tribe) became greatly terrified and looked bewildered.

devanagari: ततो निष्क्रम्य बलिन उपदेवमहाभटाः । असहन्तः तन्निनादं अभिपेतुरुदायुधाः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: tato niṣkramya balina upadevamahābhaṭāḥ asahantaḥ tanninādaṃ abhipeturudāyudhāḥ
translation: Incensed at the blast of the conch, the mighty and great warriors among the Yakṣas sallied forth from the city, and with uplifted weapons, fell upon him.

devanagari: स तान् आपततो वीर उग्रधन्वा महारथः । एकैकं युगपत्सर्वान् अहन् बाणैस्त्रिभिस्त्रिभिः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tān āpatato vīra ugradhanvā mahārathaḥ ekaikaṃ yugapatsarvān ahan bāṇaistribhistribhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active second plural], [aorist middle third single]
[imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]

translation: While the Yakṣas were dashing towards him the great warrior[2] with a formidable bow, simultaneously hit each one of them with three arrows.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
mahāratha—An expert who, riding his chariot, can engage ten thousand archers simultaneously.
eko daśa-sahasrāṇi yodhayed yas tu dhanvinām /
astra-śastra-pravīṇas'ca vijñeyaḥ sa mahārathaḥ //

devanagari: ते वै ललाटलग्नैस्तैः इषुभिः सर्व एव हि । मत्वा निरस्तमात्मानं आशंसन्कर्म तस्य तत् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: te vai lalāṭalagnaistaiḥ iṣubhiḥ sarva eva hi matvā nirastamātmānaṃ āśaṃsankarma tasya tat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: With those arrows planted on their foreheads, all of

devanagari: तेऽपि चामुममृष्यन्तः पादस्पर्शमिवोरगाः । शरैरविध्यन् युगपद् द्विगुणं प्रचिकीर्षवः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: te śarairavidhyan yugapad dviguṇaṃ pracikīrṣavaḥ
translation: Being enraged like serpents trodden under feet, the Yakṣas also, desiring to do the double of what he did, simultaneously shot at him double the number of arrows (six each).

devanagari: ततः परिघनिस्त्रिंशैः प्रासशूलपरश्वधैः । शक्त्यृष्टिभिर्भुशुण्डीभिः चित्रवाजैः शरैरपि ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ parighanistriṃśaiḥ prāsaśūlaparaśvadhaiḥ śaktyṛṣṭibhirbhuśuṇḍībhiḥ citravājaiḥ śarairapi
translation: Then fired with anger, and desirous of retaliation those one lakh and thirty-thousand Yakṣas showered on him, his chariot and charioteer iron bars, swords, darts, spears, axes, lances, double-edged swords, bhuśuṇḍi missile and arrows with variegated feathers.

devanagari: अभ्यवर्षन् प्रकुपिताः सरथं सहसारथिम् । इच्छन्तः तत्प्रतीकर्तुं अयुतानां त्रयोदश ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: abhyavarṣan prakupitāḥ sarathaṃ sahasārathim icchantaḥ tatpratīkartuṃ ayutānāṃ trayodaśa

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ iṣ class 6 verb]

translation: Then fired with anger, and desirous of retaliation those one lakh and thirty-thousand Yakṣas showered on him, his chariot and charioteer iron bars, swords, darts, spears, axes, lances, double-edged swords, bhuśuṇḍi missile and arrows with variegated feathers.

devanagari: औत्तानपादिः स तदा शस्त्रवर्षेण भूरिणा । न एवादृश्यताच्छन्न आसारेण यथा गिरिः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: auttānapādiḥ sa tadā śastravarṣeṇa bhūriṇā na evādṛśyatācchanna āsāreṇa yathā giriḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ śad class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ śad class 6 verb]

translation: At that time, being covered with the heavy volley of weapons, Dhruva, the son of Uttānapāda, could not be seen, just as a hill is hidden by a thick shower of rain.

devanagari: हाहाकारस्तदैवासीत् सिद्धानां दिवि पश्यताम् । हतोऽयं मानवः सूर्यो मग्नः पुण्यजनार्णवे ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: hāhākārastadaivāsīt siddhānāṃ divi paśyatām hato

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]
[nominative single from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ han class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ sūr class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ sūr class 10 verb]

translation: Immediately arose the cry ‘Alas! Alas’ from the Siddhas who were witnessing the fight from the sky. ‘This Sun of Manu’s race, drowned in the ocean of Yakṣa-army, is killed’.

devanagari: नदत्सु यातुधानेषु जयकाशिष्वथो मृधे । उदतिष्ठद् रथस्तस्य नीहारादिव भास्करः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: nadatsu yātudhāneṣu jayakāśiṣvatho mṛdhe udatiṣṭhad rathastasya nīhārādiva bhāskaraḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sthā class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ sthā class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sthā class 1 verb]

translation: While Yakṣas and Rākṣasas (Yātudhānas)[3] were shouting and proclaiming their victory, the chariot of Dhruva came into view, like the Sun from behind the mist.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
The Bhāgavata Purāṇa mixes up Yakṣas and Rākṣasas, as Alakā was inhabited by all kinds of demigods and their army was composed of these.

devanagari: धनुर्विस्फूर्जयन्दिव्यं द्विषतां खेदमुद्वहन् । अस्त्रौघं व्यधमद्‍बाणैः घनानीकमिवानिलः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: dhanurvisphūrjayandivyaṃ dviṣatāṃ khedamudvahan astraughaṃ vyadhamad‍bāṇaiḥ ghanānīkamivānilaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Twanging his divine bow, he struck terror in his enemies. He shattered to pieces their volley of missiles, like strong wind dispersing the army of clouds.

devanagari: तस्य ते चापनिर्मुक्ता भित्त्वा वर्माणि रक्षसाम् । कायान् आविविशुस्तिग्मा गिरीन् अशनयो यथा ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya te cāpanirmuktā bhittvā varmāṇi rakṣasām kāyān āviviśustigmā girīn aśanayo yathā
translation: Sharp arrows discharged from his bow, pierced through the armours of the Rākṣasas into their bodies, just as thunderbolts (vajras) into the mountains.

devanagari: भल्लैः सञ्छिद्यमानानां शिरोभिश्चारुकुण्डलैः । ऊरुभिर्हेमतालाभैः दोर्भिर्वलयवल्गुभिः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: bhallaiḥ sañchidyamānānāṃ śirobhiścārukuṇḍalaiḥ ūrubhirhematālābhaiḥ dorbhirvalayavalgubhiḥ
translation: When they were mowed down with Dhruva’s arrows of bhalla type, the battlefield was covered with their heads adorned with beautiful ear-rings; with their thighs resembling golden palms; with arms beautiful with bracelets; with precious necklaces, armlets, crowns and costly turbans, and it appeared attractive to warriors

devanagari: हारकेयूरमुकुटैः उष्णीषैश्च महाधनैः । आस्तृतास्ता रणभुवो रेजुर्वीरमनोहराः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: hārakeyūramukuṭaiḥ uṣṇīṣaiśca mahādhanaiḥ āstṛtāstā raṇabhuvo rejurvīramanoharāḥ
translation: When they were mowed down with Dhruva’s arrows of bhalla type, the battlefield was covered with their heads adorned with beautiful ear-rings; with their thighs resembling golden palms; with arms beautiful with bracelets; with precious necklaces, armlets, crowns and costly turbans, and it appeared attractive to warriors

devanagari: हतावशिष्टा इतरे रणाजिराद् । रक्षोगणाः क्षत्रियवर्यसायकैः । प्रायो विवृक्णावयवा विदुद्रुवुः । मृगेन्द्रविक्रीडितयूथपा इव ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: hatāvaśiṣṭā itare raṇājirād rakṣogaṇāḥ kṣatriyavaryasāyakaiḥ prāyo vivṛkṇāvayavā vidudruvuḥ mṛgendravikrīḍitayūthapā iva

translation: Most of the survivers of the Rākṣasa army who escaped death, got the limbs of their bodies cut off with the arrows of Dhruva, the foremost among the Kṣatriyas, and they

devanagari: अपश्यमानः स तदाततायिनं । महामृधे कञ्चन मानवोत्तमः । पुरीं दिदृक्षन्नपि नाविशद् द्विषां । न मायिनां वेद चिकीर्षितं जनः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: apaśyamānaḥ sa tadātatāyinaṃ mahāmṛdhe kañcana mānavottamaḥ purīṃ didṛkṣannapi nāviśad dviṣāṃ na māyināṃ veda cikīrṣitaṃ janaḥ

[nominative single from √ kṛ ], [accusative single from √ kṛ ]

translation: At that time, Dhruva, the best among Manu’s race, did not see anyone with a weapon in hand, on the great battlefield. Though he desired to see the city of the enemies, he did not enter it. ‘A man cannot know the intentions of those expert in black magic’.

devanagari: इति ब्रुवंश्चित्ररथः स्वसारथिं । यत्तः परेषां प्रतियोगशङ्कितः । शुश्राव शब्दं जलधेरिवेरितं । नभस्वतो दिक्षु रजोऽन्वदृश्यत ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: iti bruvaṃścitrarathaḥ svasārathiṃ yattaḥ pareṣāṃ pratiyogaśaṅkitaḥ śuśrāva śabdaṃ jaladheriveritaṃ nabhasvato dikṣu rajo

[nominative single from √ īr ], [accusative single from √ īr ]

translation: Telling this to his charioteer, Dhruva of wonderful chariot, apprehended an attack from the enemies and remained alert. He heard a noise like the roaring of the sea, and saw on all sides dust raised by a strong wind.

devanagari: क्षणेनाच्छादितं व्योम घनानीकेन सर्वतः । विस्फुरत्तडिता दिक्षु त्रासयत् स्तनयित्‍नुना ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: kṣaṇenācchāditaṃ vyoma ghanānīkena sarvataḥ visphurattaḍitā dikṣu trāsayat stanayit‍nunā

translation: In a moment, the sky was overcast on all sides by a host of clouds accompanied with flashes of lightening and terrible thunders rattling, in all directions

devanagari: ववृषू रुधिरौघासृक् पूयविण्मूत्रमेदसः । निपेतुर्गगनादस्य कबन्धान्यग्रतोऽनघ ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: vavṛṣū rudhiraughāsṛk pūyaviṇmūtramedasaḥ nipeturgaganādasya kabandhānyagrato
translation: They (the clouds) showered blood, phlegm, puss, excreta, fat and urine. Headless bodies fell down from heaven before him, Oh sinless (Vidura)!

devanagari: ततः खेऽदृश्यत गिरिः निपेतुः सर्वतोदिशम् । गदापरिघनिस्त्रिंश मुसलाः साश्मवर्षिणः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ khe gadāparighanistriṃśa musalāḥ sāśmavarṣiṇaḥ
translation: Then a mountain appeared in the sky; and from all directions were showered maces, iron clubs, swords, pestles along with a volley of stones.

devanagari: अहयोऽशनिनिःश्वासा वमन्तोऽग्निं रुषाक्षिभिः । अभ्यधावन् गजा मत्ताः सिंहव्याघ्राश्च यूथशः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: ahayo abhyadhāvan gajā mattāḥ siṃhavyāghrāśca yūthaśaḥ

translation: Serpents with thunderous hissing and emitting fire from eyes with rage, rushed at him. Intoxicated elephants, lions and tigers attacked him in multitudes.

devanagari: समुद्र ऊर्मिभिर्भीमः प्लावयन् सर्वतो भुवम् । आससाद महाह्रादः कल्पान्त इव भीषणः ॥ २७ ॥

[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: Terrific like the ocean at the time of deluge, at the end of a Kalpa, a loudly roaring terrible sea, engulfing the earth on all sides with waves, approached him.

devanagari: एवंविधान्यनेकानि त्रासनान्यमनस्विनाम् । ससृजुस्तिग्मगतय आसुर्या माययासुराः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃvidhānyanekāni trāsanānyamanasvinām sasṛjustigmagataya āsuryā māyayāsurāḥ
translation: By means of their special power of black magic, the Asuras (Yakṣas)of cruel disposition, created many kinds of such phenomena which strike terror into unthinking men.

devanagari: ध्रुवे प्रयुक्तामसुरैः तां मायामतिदुस्तराम् । निशम्य तस्य मुनयः शमाशंसन् समागताः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: dhruve prayuktāmasuraiḥ tāṃ māyāmatidustarām niśamya tasya munayaḥ śamāśaṃsan samāgatāḥ
translation: Hearing that insuperable type of black magic had been employed against Dhruva by the Asuras, sages assembled there praying for his welfare.

devanagari: मुनय ऊचुः । औत्तानपाद भगवान् तव शार्ङ्गधन्वा । देवः क्षिणोत्ववनतार्तिहरो विपक्षान् । यन्नामधेयमभिधाय निशम्य चाद्धा । लोकोऽञ्जसा तरति दु...
english_devnagari: munaya ūcuḥ auttānapāda bhagavān tava śārṅgadhanvā devaḥ kṣiṇotvavanatārtiharo vipakṣān yannāmadheyamabhidhāya niśamya cāddhā loko
translation:
Sages said:
Oh Dhruva, son of Uttānapāda! May the glorious Viṣṇu (wielder of the Śārṅga bow), your God who removes afflictions of the suppliants, destroy your enemies—the Lord by uttering or hearing whose name, people easily overcome death which is so very difficult to conquer.
